items.q: What does specific latent heat measure?
items.correct: The energy required to change the state of 1 kg of a substance without changing its temperature.
items.misconception: The energy required to raise the temperature of 1 kg by 1 degree.
items.random_wrong: The temperature at which a substance melts or boils.
items.why: Students confuse specific latent heat with specific heat capacity because both involve 'heat' and 'kg', missing the no-temperature-change aspect of latent heat.

items.q: Why does a metal spoon in hot water heat up faster than a wooden one?
items.correct: Metals have higher thermal conductivity, so heat is transferred more rapidly through them.
items.misconception: Metal heats up faster because it has a lower specific heat capacity, requiring less energy per degree.
items.random_wrong: Metal heats up faster because it has more thermal energy to begin with.
items.why: Students confuse the rate of heat transfer (conductivity) with how much energy a material can hold (heat capacity).

items.q: What is the difference between heat and temperature?
items.correct: Heat is the energy transferred due to temperature difference; temperature measures average kinetic energy of particles.
items.misconception: Heat and temperature are the same thing — both measure how hot something is.
items.random_wrong: Heat is measured in degrees Celsius and temperature in Joules.
items.why: Everyday language uses 'heat' and 'temperature' interchangeably ('turn up the heat'), so students don't distinguish them physically.

items.q: During boiling, temperature of the liquid:
items.why: Correct answer: Stays constant. “Rises steadily” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The answer examiners want is: J/(kg·K). “J/kg” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Energy to raise 2 kg water by 20 K? c = 4200 J/(kg·K) (kJ)?
items.why: Exact answer: 168. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
